comments.text: Canyonlands is def underrated. It’s one of the best parks and much better than grand canyon. The canyons there are incredible! Canyons in canyons! More breathtaking than Grand Canyon which is one canyon

comments.author: @dianasalazar4488
comments.text: If you want to get technical and true to history, Yosemite would have been the first national park as advocated by John Muir. President Grant marked Yellowstone as the first US park, with Yosemite becoming  national park just over a decade later.

comments.author: @Top7Daily-i9d
comments.text: What an incredible collection of America's natural wonders! Every park has its own unique beauty, and the stunning footage makes you want to start planning a road trip right away. One suggestion for future videos would be to include a few hidden gems or lesser-known spots inside each park, along with the best seasons to visit and some practical travel tips. That would make this awesome guide even more useful for travelers. Great work!

comments.author: @benjauron5873
comments.text:
You rank Death Valley and Glacier WAAAAAAY too low, and Everglades and Carlsbad Caverns WAAAAAAY too high.
And I'm not trying to dis Everglades or Carlsbad, they're both great. But better than Death Valley or Glacier? In what national park did you find the crack you're smoking?

comments.author: @MadeNewAJ
comments.text: Redwoods are too large to photograph in a regular photo. I literally had to use landscape photography to get one in. The trees are so large you could live in some of them. So fun and I miss it.

comments.text: Crater lake is under rated. 90 million trails in Crater lake? You don't give accurate information on some of these parks. But thanks it took me back to there
